name: Riverbank of the Ripoll River and "Camí dels Monjos" - circular route through the Vallès Occidental
name_original: Ribera del río Ripoll y "Camí dels Monjos" - circular por el Vallès Occidental
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cerdanyola Del Vallès offers access to diverse natural landscapes, primarily within the Parc Natural de Collserola. This region features a mix of oak and pine forests, valleys, and varied elevation changes suitable for cycling. The terrain includes both paved and unpaved surfaces, providing a range of experiences for touring cyclists. The area also incorporates historical landmarks into its route network. Best touring cycling routes around Cerdanyola Del Vallès The most popular touring cycling route is Riverbank of the Ripoll River and "Camí dels Monjos" - circular route through the Vallès Occidental, a 21.6 miles (34.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Ripoll River and explores the Vallès Occidental region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Besòs Riverside Park – Besòs River Bike Path loop from Cerdanyola del Vallès, a moderate 37.8 miles (60.9 km) path. This route offers a journey featuring riverside park scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Xandri's Pine – Cerdanyola - Forat del Vent loop from Cerdanyola del Vallès, a 24.9 miles (40.1 km) trail leading through the Collserola Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Cerdanyola Del Vallès is defined by the expansive Parc Natural de Collserola, pine forests, and valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Cerdanyola Del Vallès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 2000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cerdanyola Del Vallès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Cerdanyola del Vallès?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Cerdanyola del Vallès, offering a variety of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Cerdanyola del Vallès?
A: The terrain is quite diverse. You'll find mostly paved urban paths along the Ripoll and Besòs rivers, which are generally flat and accessible. For more challenging rides, routes through the Parc de Collserola Natural Park feature unpaved segments and significant elevation gain, often requiring good fitness.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, routes along the Ripoll and Besòs rivers are generally flat and suitable for less experienced cyclists or family outings. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e973361066/riverbank-of-the-ripoll-river-and-cami-dels-monjos-circular-route-through-the-valles-occidental" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverbank of the Ripoll River and "Camí dels Monjos"&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate route but offers accessible sections.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes through the Parc de Collserola Natural Park offer significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566322" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xandri's Pine – Cerdanyola - Forat del Vent loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that takes you through the park, as does the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canaletes Aqueduct – Cave in Parc de Collserola loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Cerdanyola del Vallès?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e973361066/riverbank-of-the-ripoll-river-and-cami-dels-monjos-circular-route-through-the-valles-occidental" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverbank of the Ripoll River and "Camí dels Monjos"&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known circular route through the Vallès Occidental. Other examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38221157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besòs Riverside Park – Bogatell Beach loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31908660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besòs Riverside Park – Besòs River Bike Path loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cerdanyola del Vallès offers access to the expansive Parc de Collserola Natural Park, known for its lush forests and diverse wildlife. You can also cycle along the Ripoll and Besòs rivers. Historical sites like the Romanesque church of Sant Iscle and Santa Victòria de les Feixes, the Iberian Museum and Village of Ca n'Oliver, and the Can Coll farmhouse (an environmental education center) can be integrated into your rides. For panoramic views, look for viewpoints within Collserola, such as the Mirador de Cerdanyola.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by bike near Cerdanyola del Vallès?
A: While the immediate area is more known for its rivers and natural park, you can find waterfalls within a reasonable cycling distance. Notable ones include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1411041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de l'Aigua Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rierada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cerdanyola del Vallès?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or cold. Summer can be warm, especially in open areas, while winter can be cooler but still suitable for cycling on many days.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes using public transport?
A: Cerdanyola del Vallès is well-connected and its proximity to Barcelona means that many routes are accessible via public transport networks. You can often reach starting points by train or bus, making it convenient to explore the region without a car.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Cerdanyola del Vallès?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene riverbanks to challenging mountain trails in Collserola, and the opportunity to combine nature with cultural and historical sights.

Q: Is the Parc de Collserola Natural Park suitable for touring cyclists?
A: Yes, the Parc de Collserola Natural Park covers a significant portion of the municipality and is a major draw for cyclists. It offers over 8,000 hectares of green space with numerous well-marked trails. While some routes involve significant elevation gain and unpaved segments, electric mountain bike tours are also available, allowing riders to enjoy the scenery more easily. You can learn more about the park at &lt;a href="https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Collserola_Natural_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikivoyage&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Passage under the road from Torre Romeu to Sabadell, following the path of the Ripoll River between Castellar and Montcada, 20 km, in the area of Barberà and Sabadell.

Bridge of the municipality of Sabadell that passes over the Ripoll river and connects the city itself with the Ermita de la Salut.
It was built between 1862 and 1863 at the time of Mayor Josep Romeu i Sans.
Historical path of medieval origin of about 27 km that links the monastery of Sant Cugat del Vallès and the monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt, located at the top of La Mola.
